channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-5055] specify mpss version in target file
item.description:
Currently the version of MPSS to build is hard-coded into the jenkins job for the branch it will build on. This is less than ideal as it requires a TEI coordination to have it updated.

The version of MPSS to build should be specified in the target file where the versions of other components are specified, such as OFED release as an example.
item.summary: specify mpss version in target file

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are closed can be reopened.

comments.comment:
How can I reproduce your attempt at this?

Ideally you should do:

$ script

and then at the shell prompt you get after the script command, show all of the steps you need to take to reproduce, including all git clones, fetches, checkouts, package downloads, etc. Assuming you have nothing already cloned, downloaded, etc. so that you show where everything comes from and what you do with it.

and when you have reproduced the above, type "exit" at your shell prompt and then provide the typescript file that the script command produced.

I have tried and encountered with many hard-coded places. As you can see from path all instances are inside MPSS SDK. Path hard-coded inside autoconf tools and perl scripts that is used by autoconf.
I'm not going to really install RPM. We just need to unpack RPMs content in this hard-coded place. It will work.

Unfortunately MPSS SDK has hard-coded path '/opt/mpss' and should be installed only in this place. So, there is very hard to find all places and replace this path to use it locally.

Have you given it a try? I personally would start with unpacking the rpm with rpm2cpio and then running a grep -r '/opt/mpss' ./opt/mpss and see what can be found (and replaced) and then try a build with that result.

Where did that libtoolize error come from? What steps did you take to produce that?

Can we make /opt/mpss directory writable for jenkins job? In this case we can install MPSS SDK automatically and use it.

Jenkins would need much more permissions than just writing to /opt/mpss to be able to install RPMs.
